FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Interface IMeasuredVirtualLayout

Namespace
FluentGpu.Scene
Assembly
FluentGpu.Scene.dll

The variable-extent (measured) virtualization seam — E11-L0. Folds the Fenwick estimate-then-correct path behind the SAME pluggable seam as the fixed-geometry layouts, so CUSTOM layouts can be variable/sliver-like: unmeasured items report their estimate; at arrange time the layout engine measures each realized row and feeds the real extent back through SetMeasured(int, float, float) (O(log n) correction), then re-pins the scroll anchor via IndexAt(float, float)/OffsetOf(int, float) so corrections above the viewport never jump the visible top (virtualization.md §6.2 — estimate-then-correct + anchoring are seam behavior now, not a special case). Built-ins: MeasuredStackVirtualLayout (the Fenwick variable list), GroupedListVirtualLayout (group headers = a measured item kind + sticky-header hook).

public interface IMeasuredVirtualLayout : IVirtualLayout
Inherited Members
IVirtualLayout.ContentExtent(int, float)
IVirtualLayout.Window(int, float, float, float, int, out int, out int)
IVirtualLayout.ItemRect(int, float)

Methods

IndexAt(float, float)

The item whose extent band contains offset — the scroll-anchor candidate.

int IndexAt(float offset, float crossSize)

Parameters

offset float
crossSize float

Returns

int

OffsetOf(int, float)

Content-space main-axis offset of item index (prefix sum over corrected extents).

float OffsetOf(int index, float crossSize)

Parameters

index int
crossSize float

Returns

float

SetMeasured(int, float, float)

Correct item index's main-axis extent to its measured value (estimate-then-correct). Called by the layout engine for every realized row at arrange time; O(log n), allocation-free.

void SetMeasured(int index, float mainExtent, float crossSize)

Parameters

index int
mainExtent float
crossSize float
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
